manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: skiing, jacks, socks, bronze, vest, platinum, train, gun, hockey.
Your opponent's team words are: ska, tequila, wine, alternative, martini, kale, zucchini, rock.
Distractor words are: guitar, plum, lime, banana, apricot, cello, harmonica.
The kill words are: hiv.

message.content:
clue.CLUE: Metal
TARGETS: bronze, platinum, gun, train, jacks
message.reasoning:
The task: provide a single-word clue and list of target words (our team's words) it relates to. Must not be any of the words in any list (including opponent/distractor/kill). Must be semantically related to the target words.

Our team words: skiing, jacks (maybe "jack"?), socks, bronze, vest, platinum, train, gun, hockey.

We should pick a clue that relates to several of these. Perhaps "sports" relates to skiing, hockey, gun? Not that. "Winter" relates to skiing, hockey (ice), socks? "Metal" relates to bronze, platinum, gun? Also "metal" is a distractor? Not listed. Metal is not in any list. It could relate to bronze, platinum, gun (metal weapons), maybe jacks (metal object), train (metal). That's many. But also "metal" could be ambiguous with "metal music" which is not opponent words (rock is opponent). But "rock" is opponent word, but "metal" is not rock but a genre. Could be okay.

But we must ensure clue is not part of any list: metal is not in any list. Good.

So CLUE: Metal

Now which target words? Let's decide: bronze, platinum, gun, train, jacks. Possibly also skiing? Not metal? Could be "ski" made of metal edges. But maybe too weak. Socks not metal. Vest could be bulletproof vest (kevlar). Could be metal? Not.

Our clue must be semantically related to the target words we list. So we need to list which ones we think the partner will guess. Probably the more we list the more chance to hit opponent or distractor. Let's be safe with maybe 4 or 5.

Potential set: bronze, platinum, gun, train, jacks.

Check none of those are kill/opponent/distractor. They are our team words. Good.

Now ensure "metal" does not contain part of any word (like "train" contains "rain" no). It's fine.

Return format:

CLUE: Metal
TARGETS: bronze, platinum, gun, train, jacks

That's a single word
